changes.human_diff: 'fact' renamed to 'extracted_text'. All consumers referencing the old name will break.
'confidence' maximum changed from 0.9 to 100.0 — scale is now 0–100 instead of 0.0–1.0. CRITICAL: downstream ranking inflated by 100×.

failure_modes.description: Consumer references the old field name which is now absent.
Confidence values are now 0–100 instead of 0.0–1.0. Consumers applying a threshold of > 0.7 will now always pass (100 > 0.7), silently accepting all facts regardless of quality.
failure_modes.impact: All downstream quality gates based on confidence threshold become no-ops.

failure_modes.description: Confidence values are now 0–100 instead of 0.0–1.0. Consumers applying a threshold of > 0.7 will now always pass (100 > 0.7), silently accepting all facts regardless of quality.
failure_modes.impact: All downstream quality gates based on confidence threshold become no-ops.

migration_checklist: IMMEDIATE: Halt all downstream deployments — CRITICAL breaking change detected
IMMEDIATE: Notify all 2 affected consumer team(s) of CRITICAL schema change
Freeze schema in production until migration is complete
Identify all consumers reading affected fields: ['confidence', 'fact']
CRITICAL FIX: Rescale confidence values — divide all values by 100 to restore 0.0–1.0 range before deploying to consumers
Add contract validation gate: max(confidence) <= 1.0 to CI pipeline
Add backward-compat alias: expose 'fact' alongside 'extracted_text' during migration window
Set a deprecation deadline for old field names (recommend: 30 days)
Deploy schema change behind a feature flag — enable for one consumer at a time
Run ValidationRunner against new snapshot: python contracts/runner.py --contract-id ...
Validate downstream pipeline outputs against expected distributions
Monitor for anomalies 24 h post-deployment before enabling for all consumers
Remove deprecated aliases / tombstoned fields after migration window closes
rollback_plan: Restore previous schema snapshot by reverting the snapshot directory to last known-good commit
Run: git revert <offending-commit> --no-edit  (do NOT force-push to main)
Re-run ValidationRunner against restored snapshot to confirm it passes all checks
Replay affected pipelines from the last valid checkpoint
Notify downstream consumers that the rollback is complete and stable
Open a post-mortem issue documenting the root cause and adding a CI guard to prevent recurrence
